prompt:
-- stdlib/bpe.rail — Byte-pair encoding tokenizer for Rail LM training.
-- Import: import "stdlib/bpe.rail"
--
-- BPE learns sub-word merge rules from a corpus, compressing long
-- character sequences into fewer tokens. The v2.3 char-level tokenizer
-- (stdlib/tokenizer.rail) caps training seq-length at corpus char count;
-- BPE at target_vocab=4096 compresses ~3x on Rail source, unlocking
-- chunked training on the 553K stdlib corpus.
--
-- Usage:
--   import "stdlib/bpe.rail"
--   let bpe = bpe_train corpus 512
--   let ids = bpe_encode bpe corpus
--   let back = bpe_decode bpe ids           -- == corpus
--   let _ = bpe_save "/tmp/tok" bpe          -- → /tmp/tok.vocab + .merges
--   let bpe2 = bpe_load "/tmp/tok"
--
-- Layout:
--   BPE merges vocab size
--     merges : [(a, b, new_id)]  — ordered learned merges
--     vocab  : [string]          — byte-sequence per token id (base chars
--                                  first, merged tokens appended in order)
--     size   : int               — len vocab
--
-- The base vocab is the set of distinct characters in the training corpus
-- (first-appearance order, same discipline as stdlib/tokenizer.rail).
-- Merged tokens start at id = base_size. Encoding applies merges greedy
-- left-to-right in learn order; decoding concatenates vocab[i] byte-seqs.
completion:
bpe_pair_key a b = a * 1048576 + b
bpe_key_a k = k / 1048576
bpe_key_b k = k - (k / 1048576) * 1048576
bpe_nth n xs =
bpe_drop n xs =
bpe_contains_char cs c =
bpe_collect_unique input acc =
bpe_char_id_loop cs c i =
bpe_init_encode_loop cs vocab_list acc =
bpe_count_pairs tokens m =

prompt: bpe_vocab_size bpe = match bpe
completion: | BPE _ _ s -> s

-- stdlib/map.rail — Pure Rail tree map (BST)
-- O(log n) average insert/lookup, O(n) worst case (unbalanced)
-- Supports string and integer keys via Rail's polymorphic ==, <, >
completion:
map_new _ = MapEmpty
map_put m k v = match m
map_get m k = match m
map_has m k = match m
map_del m k = match m
map_merge l r = match l
map_pop_min m = match m
map_keys m = match m
map_size m = match m

prompt: map_keys m = match m
completion:
| MapEmpty -> []
  | MapNode k v l r -> append (map_keys l) (cons k (map_keys r))

prompt:
-- Top-k sampling: find the k largest probabilities, renormalise over
-- them, sample one.  k must be ≥ 1.  For k=1 this reduces to argmax.
-- The simple O(V·k) selection is fine for small vocabs (≤256).
find_kth_largest probs V k =
completion: find_kth_loop probs V k 0.0 0

prompt:
-- First iteration (iters=0): ignore prev_max, take global max.
-- Later iterations: take largest value strictly less than prev_max.
max_below probs V prev_max i best_v iters =
completion:
if i >= V then best_v
  else
    let v = float_arr_get probs i
    let qualifies = if iters == 0 then true else v < prev_max
    if qualifies then
      if v > best_v then max_below probs V prev_max (i + 1) v iters
      else max_below probs V prev_max (i + 1) best_v iters
    else max_below probs V prev_max (i + 1) best_v iters

prompt: tensor_softmax t = match t
completion:
| Tensor t_data t_shape t_st ->
    let n = list_product t_shape
    let cols = if length t_shape >= 1 then head (reverse t_shape) else n
    let rows = n / cols
    if gpu_available 0 then
      Tensor (gpu_softmax_ffi t_data rows cols) t_shape t_st
    else
      let c = tensor_zeros t_shape
      match c | Tensor c_data _ _ ->
        let _ = softmax_rows t_data c_data rows cols 0
        c

prompt: tensor_one_hot t vocab_size = match t
completion:
| Tensor t_data t_shape _ ->
    let batch = list_product t_shape
    let out = tensor_zeros (cons batch (cons vocab_size []))
    match out | Tensor o_data _ _ ->
      let _ = one_hot_loop t_data o_data batch vocab_size 0
      out

  let _ = tensor_set_flat t i (orig - grad_eps)
  let fm = sum_exp_grad t
  let _ = tensor_set_flat t i orig
  (fp - fm) / (2.0 * grad_eps)

prompt: drop_n n cs =

-- ── Initialize weights (small random-ish values) ──
init_w1 _ =

-- Test one edge case file, harvest if it fails
test_file path =
completion:
let name = trim (shell (str_cat ["basename ", path, " .rail"]))
  let code = read_file path
  if length code < 10 then 0
  else
    let _ = print (str_cat ["  ", name, " (", show (length code), " chars)"])
    let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_gengap_test.rail" code
    let compile_out = shell "cd ~/projects/rail && ./rail_native /tmp/rail_gengap_test.rail 2>&1"
    let ok = trim (shell "test -f /tmp/rail_out && echo ok || echo fail")
    if ok == "fail" then
      let _ = print "    COMPILE FAIL"
      let prompt = str_cat ["This Rail program fails to compile:\n\n", code, "\n\nCompiler output: ", trim compile_out, "\n\nExplain why it fails and what the compiler needs to handle it."]
      let resp = ask_claude prompt
      if length resp > 20 then
        let _ = write_entry prompt resp
        1
      else 0
    else
      let _ = shell "perl -e 'alarm 5; exec @ARGV' /tmp/rail_out > /tmp/rail_gengap_stdout.txt 2>&1; echo $? > /tmp/rail_gengap_exit.txt"
      let exit_code = trim (read_file "/tmp/rail_gengap_exit.txt")
      if exit_code == "0" then
        let output = trim (read_file "/tmp/rail_gengap_stdout.txt")
        let _ = print (str_cat ["    ok: ", output])
        0
      else
        let _ = print (str_cat ["    RUNTIME FAIL exit=", exit_code])
        let rt = shell "sed -n '/^rt_core/,/^runtime_asm/p' tools/compile.rail | head -80"
        let prompt = str_cat ["This Rail program compiles but crashes (exit ", exit_code, "):\n\n", code, "\n\nRuntime:\n", rt, "\n\nDiagnose the root cause and propose a fix."]
        let resp = ask_claude prompt
        if length resp > 20 then
          let _ = write_entry prompt resp
          1
        else 0

prompt: harvest_gap_list files count =
completion:
if length files == 0 then
    let _ = print (str_cat ["  Harvested ", show count, " gap examples"])
    count
  else
    let path = trim (head files)
    if length path < 5 then harvest_gap_list (tail files) count
    else
      let name = trim (shell (str_cat ["basename ", path, " .md"]))
      let _ = print (str_cat ["\n  Gap: ", name])
      -- Write gap to temp for sed extraction
      let content = read_file path
      let _ = write_file "/tmp/rail_harvest_gap.txt" content
      let code = extract_between "/tmp/rail_harvest_gap.txt" "RAILCODE" "ENDRAILCODE"
      let error = extract_between "/tmp/rail_harvest_gap.txt" "## Error" "## Code"
      if length code < 5 then
        let _ = print "    skip: no code"
        harvest_gap_list (tail files) count
      else
        let rt = shell "sed -n '/^rt_core/,/^runtime_asm/p' tools/compile.rail | head -120"
        -- Variant 1: Full repair prompt
        let prompt1 = str_cat ["This Rail program crashes with ", trim error, ":\n\n", code, "\n\nThe compiler runtime:\n", rt, "\n\nDiagnose the root cause and propose a minimal fix using OLD:/ENDOLD/NEW:/ENDNEW markers."]
        let resp1 = ask_claude prompt1
        let _ = if length resp1 > 20 then write_entry prompt1 resp1 else 0
        -- Variant 2: Just diagnosis (no patch request)
        let prompt2 = str_cat ["This Rail program crashes with ", trim error, ":\n\n", code, "\n\nExplain in one paragraph why this crashes and what the compiler would need to handle it."]
        let resp2 = ask_claude prompt2
        let _ = if length resp2 > 20 then write_entry prompt2 resp2 else 0
        -- Variant 3: Different framing
        let prompt3 = str_cat ["A user reports their Rail program segfaults:\n\n", code, "\n\nThe compiler produces ARM64 native binaries. Error: ", trim error, ". What is the likely cause and how should the compiler be fixed?"]
        let resp3 = ask_claude prompt3
        let _ = if length resp3 > 20 then write_entry prompt3 resp3 else 0
        let new_count = count + 3
        harvest_gap_list (tail files) new_count

prompt:
-- Multi-candidate racing: for each task, try up to 3 candidates at the given temperature
-- First candidate that compiles + passes gets harvested
try_gen_multi sys port tasks harvested temp =
completion:
if length tasks == 0 then harvested
  else
    let m = arena_mark 0
    let task_line = head tasks
    let task = bench_task_desc task_line
    let expected = bench_task_expected task_line
    let hint = if expected == "" then "" else cat ["\n\nThe program must print exactly: ", expected]
    let user = cat [task, hint, "\n\nOutput ONLY Rail code. No markdown, no explanation. Functions before main."]
    let got = try_candidates sys port user task hint expected temp 1
    let _ = arena_reset m
    try_gen_multi sys port (tail tasks) (harvested + got) temp

  write_file state_path json_str

  let pe = build_pe seq d

  next_from_probs probs_t seq V

prompt:
-- lm_mh.rail — multi-head transformer LM with learnable LayerNorm.
--
-- v2.12 upgrade over v2.5 (lm_transformer.rail):
--   • Multi-head causal attention (4 heads × head_dim=8, d_model=32)
--   • Learnable LayerNorm γ,β (dgamma/dbeta + Adam state)
--   • Separate attention output projection W_AO (d→d) and
--     language-modeling head W_LM (d→V)
--
-- Weights list layout (index → name):
--   0 w_e    1 w_q    2 w_k    3 w_v    4 w_ao
--   5 w_f1   6 w_f2   7 w_lm   8 ln1_g  9 ln1_b
--  10 ln2_g 11 ln2_b
-- States list mirrors weights (same indices).
-- Config: [seq, V, d, d_ff, n_heads, head_dim].
--
-- Goal: beat v2.3 bigram baseline (loss 2.10).
completion:
corpus _ =
init_weight arr n seed scale = init_w_loop arr n seed scale 0
init_w_loop arr n seed scale i =
ones_loop arr n i =
xavier_scale fan_in fan_out =
make_weight rows cols seed =
make_gamma d =
make_beta d =
fill_onehot dst ids V seq i =
fill_labels arr ids seq i =

prompt: ce_grad_loop dst probs labels seq V invN i =
completion:
if i >= seq * V then 0
  else
    let row = i / V
    let col = i % V
    let target = float_to_int (float_arr_get labels row)
    let p = float_arr_get probs i
    let g = if col == target then (p - 1.0) * invN else p * invN
    let _ = float_arr_set dst i g
    ce_grad_loop dst probs labels seq V invN (i + 1)

prompt:
-- ── Forward pass.  ws = weight list; cfg = [seq, V, d, d_ff, n_heads, head_dim]; pe = raw float_arr ──
-- Activations returned as a list (slot → name):
--   0 probs     1 x_blk    2 ln2_out   3 x_attn    4 ln1_out   5 x_pe
--   6 Q         7 K        8 V         9 concat_attn  10 ffn_h   11 relu_mask
--  12 ln1_mean 13 ln1_rstd 14 ln2_mean 15 ln2_rstd 16 mh_per_head
forward x ws cfg pe =
completion:
let seq      = list_nth 0 cfg
  let d        = list_nth 2 cfg
  let d_ff     = list_nth 3 cfg
  let n_heads  = list_nth 4 cfg
  let head_dim = list_nth 5 cfg
  let w_e    = list_nth 0 ws
  let w_q    = list_nth 1 ws
  let w_k    = list_nth 2 ws
  let w_v    = list_nth 3 ws
  let w_ao   = list_nth 4 ws
  let w_f1   = list_nth 5 ws
  let w_f2   = list_nth 6 ws
  let w_lm   = list_nth 7 ws
  let ln1_g  = list_nth 8 ws
  let ln1_b  = list_nth 9 ws
  let ln2_g  = list_nth 10 ws
  let ln2_b  = list_nth 11 ws
  let e = matmul x w_e
  let ed = unwrap e
  let _ = add_buf_inplace ed pe (seq * d) 0
  let x_pe = e
  let ln1_pack = layernorm_save x_pe ln1_g ln1_b
  let ln1_out  = head ln1_pack
  let ln1_mean = head (tail ln1_pack)
  let ln1_rstd = head (tail (tail ln1_pack))
  let q = matmul ln1_out w_q
  let k = matmul ln1_out w_k
  let v = matmul ln1_out w_v
  let mh_pack = mh_forward q k v seq d n_heads head_dim
  let concat_attn = head mh_pack
  let mh_per_head = head (tail mh_pack)
  let attn_proj = matmul concat_attn w_ao
  let x_attn = tensor_add x_pe attn_proj
  let ln2_pack = layernorm_save x_attn ln2_g ln2_b
  let ln2_out  = head ln2_pack
  let ln2_mean = head (tail ln2_pack)
  let ln2_rstd = head (tail (tail ln2_pack))
  let ffn_h_pre = matmul ln2_out w_f1
  let ffn_h_pre_d = unwrap ffn_h_pre
  let ffn_h_data = float_arr_new (seq * d_ff) 0.0
  let ffn_mask = float_arr_new (seq * d_ff) 0.0
  let _ = relu_fwd ffn_h_pre_d ffn_h_data ffn_mask (seq * d_ff) 0
  let ffn_h = Tensor ffn_h_data (cons seq (cons d_ff [])) (compute_strides (cons seq (cons d_ff [])))
  let ffn_out = matmul ffn_h w_f2
  let x_blk = tensor_add x_attn ffn_out
  let logits = matmul x_blk w_lm
  let probs = tensor_softmax logits
  let a0  = cons mh_per_head []
  let a1  = cons ln2_rstd a0
  let a2  = cons ln2_mean a1
  let a3  = cons ln1_rstd a2
  let a4  = cons ln1_mean a3
  let a5  = cons ffn_mask a4
  let a6  = cons ffn_h a5
  let a7  = cons concat_attn a6
  let a8  = cons v a7
  let a9  = cons k a8
  let a10 = cons q a9
  let a11 = cons x_pe a10
  let a12 = cons ln1_out a11
  let a13 = cons x_attn a12
  let a14 = cons ln2_out a13
  let a15 = cons x_blk a14
  cons probs a15

prompt:
-- ── Training step ──
train_step x labels ws states cfg pe dlog_buf lr =
completion:
let act = forward x ws cfg pe
  let probs = list_nth 0 act
  let V = list_nth 1 cfg
  let seq = list_nth 0 cfg
  let loss = ce_loss probs labels seq V
  let grads = backward x labels ws cfg act dlog_buf
  let _ = apply_adam_loop ws grads states lr 0
  loss

prompt: linear_relu x w b =
completion:
let y = linear2 x w b
  tensor_relu y

prompt: ce_grad_loop dst probs labels N C invN i =
completion:
if i >= N * C then 0
  else
    let row = i / C
    let col = i % C
    let target = float_to_int (float_arr_get labels row)
    let p = float_arr_get probs i
    let g = if col == target then (p - 1.0) * invN else p * invN
    let _ = float_arr_set dst i g
    ce_grad_loop dst probs labels N C invN (i + 1)

prompt: corpus _ =
completion:
cat ["To be, or not to be, that is the question.
",
       "Whether tis nobler in the mind to suffer
",
       "the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune,
",
       "or to take arms against a sea of troubles
",
       "and by opposing end them. To die, to sleep,
",
       "no more; and by a sleep to say we end
",
       "the heart-ache and the thousand natural shocks
",
       "that flesh is heir to: tis a consummation
",
       "devoutly to be wished. To die, to sleep;
"]

prompt: unwrap t
completion: match t | Tensor d _ _ -> d

prompt: relu_fwd src dst mask n i =
completion:
if i >= n then 0
  else
    let v = float_arr_get src i
    let pos = if v > 0.0 then 1.0 else 0.0
    let _ = float_arr_set mask i pos
    let _ = float_arr_set dst i (v * pos)
    relu_fwd src dst mask n (i + 1)

prompt: ce_loss probs labels seq V = match probs
completion:
| Tensor pd _ _ -> match labels
    | Tensor ld _ _ ->
      let acc = float_arr_new 1 0.0
      let _ = lm_ce_loss_loop pd ld seq V acc 0
      (float_arr_get acc 0) / int_to_float seq

prompt: init_list xs
completion: if length xs <= 1 then [] else cons (head xs) (init_list (tail xs))
